steps.title: Use the contrast to narrow the meaning
steps.content:
The passage opens with Unlike Kuroda Seiki, who adopted traditional European methods — so Hishida did the opposite. The blank must mean something like “actively used” or “accepted” traditional Japanese approaches. The example confirms this: Hishida produced the work using color pigments on silk scroll, traditional Japanese materials.
  • Choice A — ‘distrusted’: Implies Hishida was suspicious of traditional approaches — the opposite of what the example shows. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘embraced’: Means to actively accept and use something; fits perfectly with Hishida choosing traditional silk and pigments. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘released’: Means to let go or give up — implies Hishida abandoned traditional approaches, not adopted them. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘overlooked’: Means to ignore or fail to notice — again implies Hishida did not engage with traditional approaches. ✗

steps.title: Select the matching choice
steps.content: “Embraced” is the only word that means actively adopting and using traditional Japanese methods, which is exactly what the example of silk and pigments confirms. That matches choice B — B is correct.
